title: Operational zones, SLA, capacity and fulfillment modes

slices.name: Zone creation, editing and geofence boundary ownership
evidence: dsh_platform_zones remains the canonical operational-zone owner.
Governed service-area geofences remain the spatial boundary owner; no competing geometry model was introduced.
Zone active state, versions, audit events and rollback feed the unified operational decision.

slices.name: Store and location serviceability
evidence: EvaluateOperationalPolicyForStore resolves each store through canonical service_area_code to its governed zone.
Existing client-address geofence validation remains authoritative for address-to-service-area matching.
No local frontend serviceability truth was introduced.

slices.name: Preparation, assignment and delivery SLA
evidence: Migration adds max_assignment_mins with bounded database validation.
Operational profiles manage preparation, assignment and delivery limits using expected-version guards.
The canonical decision returns all three SLA limits and their governing version.

slices.name: Capacity, pressure, pause and resume
evidence: Capacity configuration owns is_paused and pause_reason.
Live non-terminal order count is evaluated against maximum orders and throttle threshold.
Paused, exhausted and throttled states fail closed before protected mutations.

slices.name: BThwani delivery, partner delivery and client pickup
evidence: dsh_platform_delivery_mode_policies owns bthwani_delivery, partner_delivery and client_pickup per zone.
Legacy pickup input is normalized to client_pickup without creating a second source of truth.
Client pickup prevents BThwani dispatch while partner delivery preserves partner handoff requirements.

slices.name: Store onboarding fee policy and reference
evidence: The existing DSH store-onboarding-fee policy remains the definition and reference owner.
JRN-029 audit rollback supports the existing policy aggregate.
No WLT wallet balance or ledger mutation was added.

slices.name: Effects on cart, checkout, order and dispatch
evidence: OperationalPolicyEffectsMiddleware is mounted in executable composition before mutation handlers.
Cart items, serviceability, checkout intents, order creation and dispatch assignments consume the same store decision.
Denied effects return OPERATIONAL_POLICY_DENIED and never reach the owning mutation handler.

slices.name: Audit, versions and rollback
evidence: Every governed mutation requires actor, surface, reason, idempotency key and correlation ID.
Append-only events cover zone, SLA, capacity, delivery mode and onboarding-fee aggregates.
Rollback restores a prior snapshot only through a new expected-version-guarded mutation and emits rolled_back evidence.
